senses.definition: a class of animals to which humans, cows, lions, etc. belong, have warm blood, fur or hair and typically produce milk to feed their young

examples.x: Elephants, with their distinctive trunks and large ears, are among the most iconic mammals found in Africa and Asia.
Whales, dolphins, and porpoises are all examples of marine mammals.
Humans are classified as mammals because they nurse their young.

senses.definition: any cold-blooded animal with the ability to live both on land and in water, such as toads, frogs, etc.

examples.x: The frog is a well-known amphibian that lays its eggs in water and undergoes metamorphosis from tadpole to adult.
Salamanders are amphibians that have a slender body and long tail, often found near freshwater habitats.
Toads are amphibians similar to frogs but with dry, warty skin and shorter hind legs, adapted for hopping rather than leaping.
Newts are small, brightly colored amphibians that typically inhabit ponds and streams, where they breed and lay eggs.
Some amphibians, such as the African clawed frog, are commonly kept as pets in home aquariums.

senses.definition: any mammal that lays eggs and is only found in Australia, such as platypus
any of the order of mammals that carry their young babies in a pouch, such as kangaroos, found either in Australia or Americas
a class of terrestrial arthropods that breathe air and have four pairs of limbs, such as spiders, scorpions, etc.
a small creature such as a bee or ant that has six legs, and generally one or two pairs of wings

examples.x: I was observing an insect crawling on the tree bark.
Insects have six legs and a segmented body.
Mark used a magnifying glass to examine the intricate details of the insect.
Sarah was amazed by the intricate patterns on the insect's wings.
The butterfly is a colorful and beautiful insect.

senses.definition: an animal with a beak, wings, and feathers that is usually capable of flying

examples.x: I saw a bird pecking at the ground searching for food.
The bird built a cozy nest in the tree to lay its eggs.
The bird had bright feathers and a long beak.
The colorful bird flew high in the sky.
We enjoyed hearing the bird's melodic song from afar.

senses.definition: an animal with a tail, gills and fins that lives in water

examples.x: My uncle and I went snorkeling and saw beautiful tropical fish underwater.
The colorful fish in the aquarium captured my attention.
We saw a group of fish swimming together near the coral reef.

senses.definition: any type of small insect

examples.x: There was a tiny bug crawling on the kitchen counter.
She hates bugs and always keeps insect repellent with her.
A bug flew into the room through the open window.
The picnic was ruined by annoying bugs buzzing around.
Some bugs are harmless, while others can be dangerous.

senses.definition: a class of animals to which crocodiles, lizards, etc. belong, characterized by having cold blood and scaly skin

examples.x: A reptile like a snake can survive in a wide range of environments.
Crocodiles are one of the most dangerous species of reptiles in the wild.
The zoo has a variety of reptiles, including lizards, turtles, and snakes.
The reptile crawled slowly across the sunlit rock, soaking up warmth.
Reptiles are cold-blooded and rely on external heat sources to regulate their body temperature.

senses.definition: any small mammal with a pair of strong front teeth, such as mice, hamsters, rats, etc.

examples.x: The house mouse is a common rodent found in urban and rural areas around the world.
Rats, another type of rodent, are known for their intelligence and adaptability to diverse environments.
Squirrels, although often considered cute, are also rodents that play crucial roles in forest ecosystems.
Guinea pigs, while resembling rodents, are actually classified in a separate family known as cavies.
Porcupines, although not commonly thought of as rodents, are classified in the rodent family and are known for their quills used as defense mechanisms.

senses.definition: any of the mammals that are categorized by thick skin and large hooves, such as elephants, rhinoceroses and hippopotami
any bottom-dwelling marine fish with a flat body, a color similar to the seabed and both eyes on the upper side of the body
an aquatic animal that feeds at the bottom of a lake, river, etc.
a bird, such as an eagle or a hawk, that feeds on animal flesh
any bird that makes a musical call
a large female insect that is fertile and can lay eggs in an insect colony
a strong mammal used for carrying heavy loads, also known as a beast of burden
an animal that feeds on the flesh of other animals

examples.x: Lions are powerful carnivores that hunt in prides.
Wolves are social carnivores that hunt in packs.
Domestic cats are obligate carnivores, needing meat to thrive.
Polar bears are apex carnivores of the Arctic, preying on seals.
Hyenas are scavenging carnivores known for their distinctive laughs.

senses.definition: any animal that only feeds on plants

examples.x: Deer and rabbits are herbivores that graze on grass and leaves.
Elephants are large herbivores that consume vast amounts of vegetation.
Cows, essential herbivores in agriculture, chew cud to digest fibrous plant material.
Many herbivores have specialized digestive systems for breaking down plant material.
Giraffes use their long necks to reach leaves high in trees, typical of herbivores.

senses.definition: an animal or person that feeds on a wide range of both plants and meat

examples.x: The bear is a classic example of an omnivore, as it consumes both berries and fish.
Humans are omnivores, enjoying a varied diet that includes fruits, vegetables, and various types of meat.
The panda is a well-known omnivore, consuming bamboo shoots as well as small animals.

senses.definition: an animal that primarily feeds on insects or other small invertebrates
a bird that is hunted by people either for sport or food
any tropical bird, the male of which is colorful and displays unique courtship skills, originated in Papua New Guinea and Australia
any bird that migrates regularly from a region to another in different seasons of the year
a bird that lives near the sea
any aquatic bird, especially a game bird of fresh waters
small animals or insects that are believed to be destructive and are difficult to handle when they appear in a considerable number

examples.x: After the flood, the basement was infested with vermin such as rats and cockroaches.
The farmer set traps around the field to protect his crops from the vermin that were eating the seeds.
In many big cities, pigeons are often considered vermin due to their large populations and the mess they create.
The old warehouse was condemned after inspectors found it overrun with vermin.
During the medieval times, vermin like fleas and lice were rampant and often spread diseases.

senses.definition: any animal that lives by hunting and eating other animals

examples.x: The lion is a formidable predator, using its strength and agility to hunt large herbivores such as zebras and wildebeests.
The African savanna is home to a diverse range of predators, including lions, cheetahs, and hyenas.
Tigers, as solitary predators, stalk their prey silently through the dense forests of Asia.
Sharks are formidable predators of the ocean, with sleek bodies and rows of sharp teeth.
Jaguars, with powerful jaws and keen senses, are top predators in the dense rainforests of South America.

senses.definition: animals or birds that are hunted for food or sport
an invertebrate animal with a segmented body and a chitinous exoskeleton, such as a spider, crab, etc.

examples.x: Arthropods like ants and beetles are found in almost every habitat.
She collected colorful butterflies, which are arthropods.
Crustaceans, including shrimp and lobsters, are aquatic arthropods.
Spiders are arthropods known for their silk-spinning abilities.
Arthropods molt their exoskeletons as they grow larger.

senses.definition: species that do not possess or cannot develop a spinal column, such as an arthropod, mollusk, etc.

examples.x: Invertebrates like butterflies and ants are vital to garden ecosystems.
Crabs and lobsters are marine invertebrates with hard exoskeletons.
The pond teemed with tiny invertebrates that served as food for larger fish.
Spiders are arachnids, a type of invertebrate that spins webs to catch prey.
She studied various invertebrates in biology class, including earthworms and jellyfish.

senses.definition: a large group of animals characterized by the possession of a backbone or spinal column, including mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians, and fishes
turkeys, chickens, geese, ducks, etc. that are kept for their eggs and meat

examples.x: The farm specializes in raising poultry for both meat and egg production.
She bought fresh poultry from the local market for dinner.
Poultry farming is an important agricultural industry in many countries.
The chef prepared a delicious dish using tender poultry and seasonal vegetables.
He enjoys raising poultry in his backyard as a hobby.

senses.definition: (of wild animals) living by preying on other animals, especially live animals

examples.x: The cheetah's predatory instincts kicked in as it spotted a herd of antelope.
Hawks have sharp talons and keen eyesight, perfect for their predatory lifestyle.
The predatory fish lurked in the shadows, waiting for an opportunity to strike.
The owl's predatory gaze followed the movement of a mouse on the forest floor.

senses.definition: an animal that is hunted and eaten by another animal
a domesticated bird that is particularly kept for its meat and eggs

examples.x: The farmyard was bustling with fowl of all shapes and sizes.
Poultry farmers raise various types of fowl for meat and eggs.
She enjoys sketching different fowl in their natural habitats.
Wild fowl, such as ducks and geese, migrate to warmer climates during winter.

senses.definition: any large wild animal of the cat family, such as lions, tigers, etc.
a group of mammals, including cows, buffalo, bison, and antelopes, that are characterized by cloven hooves and ruminant digestion
a quadruped mammal with a slender build and curved horns that is native to Africa or Eurasia
any mammalian animal that belongs to the same group as humans, such as monkeys, apes, lemurs, etc.
a quadruped mammal that is herbivorous and has a complex digestive system from which it can bring the food back to its mouth and chew it again
an organism, typically an insect or a tick, that transmits a pathogen or parasite from one host to another
a domestic animal that is lost, abandoned or wandering without an owner
an animal with four legs, such as dogs, cats, horses, and cows
a type of marine invertebrate that can change its shape and form through a process called polymorphism
an animal, typically an insect or a bird, that transfers pollen from one flower to another
an insect or small animal that destroys or damages crops, food, etc.
any invertebrate that lives in aquatic or damp habitats and has a soft unsegmented body, often covered with a shell

examples.x: The beach was scattered with empty mollusk shells.
Octopuses are intelligent mollusks capable of changing color and shape.
She collected seashells, fascinated by the diversity of mollusks.
Clams and mussels are edible mollusks found in coastal waters.
Some mollusks, like snails, move slowly using a muscular foot.

senses.definition: a type of mollusk with a single, usually spirally coiled shell, a muscular foot, and a head with sensory tentacles and a mouth
a fish that can not survive in salty waters and usually lives in lakes or rivers
a fish that can survive in salty water and lives in oceans, also known as marine fish
a fish that can survive the salty water of the sea
all wild animals, considered as a whole, living in the natural environment

examples.x: The national park is home to a variety of wildlife, including bears and wolves.
Conservation efforts are important for protecting endangered wildlife.
The jungle is teeming with wildlife, from insects to large mammals.
He enjoys watching wildlife documentaries in his spare time.
The government has enacted laws to protect local wildlife.
